assertion_results.text: Each task file contains all required template sections: Repository, Target Branch, Description, at least one of Files to Modify or Files to Create, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements
assertion_results.evidence: All 6 task files were inspected. Each contains: Repository (e.g., 'trustify-backend'), Target Branch ('main'), Description (multi-sentence paragraph), at least one of Files to Modify or Files to Create, Implementation Notes (with bullet points referencing code), Acceptance Criteria (checkbox lists), and Test Requirements (checkbox lists). Task 1 has Files to Create and Files to Modify. Task 2 has Files to Modify only. Task 3 has both. Task 4 has Files to Modify only. Task 5 has Files to Create and Files to Modify. Task 6 has Files to Modify only.

assertion_results.text: Task count is between 3 and 10 inclusive
assertion_results.evidence: There are exactly 6 task files: task-1-advisory-severity-summary-model.md, task-2-advisory-summary-service.md, task-3-advisory-summary-endpoint.md, task-4-cache-invalidation.md, task-5-integration-tests.md, task-6-threshold-query-param.md. 6 is between 3 and 10 inclusive.
All 6 tasks have '## Repository' followed by 'trustify-backend': task-1 line 4, task-2 line 4, task-3 line 4, task-4 line 4, task-5 line 4, task-6 line 4.

assertion_results.text: Task dependencies form a DAG with no circular dependencies
assertion_results.evidence: Dependency graph: Task 1 has no dependencies. Task 2 depends on Task 1. Task 3 depends on Tasks 1, 2. Task 4 depends on Task 3. Task 5 depends on Task 3. Task 6 depends on Tasks 3, 5. This forms a directed acyclic graph: 1 -> 2 -> 3 -> {4, 5 -> 6}. No cycles exist — all edges point from lower-numbered tasks to higher-numbered tasks, making cycles structurally impossible.

assertion_results.text: Every task's Files to Modify and Files to Create reference paths plausible within the repo-backend.md structure
assertion_results.evidence: Checked each task against repo-backend.md directory tree. Task 1: Creates modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/advisory_summary.rs (new file in existing model/ dir), modifies modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/mod.rs (exists in tree). Task 2: Modifies modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs (exists in tree). Task 3: Creates modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/advisory_summary.rs (new file in existing endpoints/ dir), modifies modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/mod.rs (exists in tree). Task 4: Modifies modules/ingestor/src/graph/advisory/mod.rs (exists in tree). Task 5: Creates tests/api/advisory_summary.rs (new file in existing tests/api/ dir), modifies tests/Cargo.toml (exists in tree). Task 6: Modifies modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/advisory_summary.rs (created by Task 3) and modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs (exists in tree). All paths are consistent with the repo-backend.md directory structure.

assertion_results.text: Impact map lists concrete, specific changes (not vague or abstract descriptions)
assertion_results.evidence: The impact-map.md lists 7 specific changes under trustify-backend: 'Add AdvisorySeveritySummary response model struct with fields: critical, high, medium, low, total', 'Add advisory_summary method to SbomService that queries the sbom_advisory join table, joins to the advisory table, deduplicates by advisory ID, and groups counts by severity', 'Add GET /api/v2/sbom/{id}/advisory-summary endpoint handler...', 'Register the new endpoint route in the SBOM endpoints module with 5-minute tower-http cache configuration', 'Add cache invalidation logic in the advisory ingestion pipeline...', 'Support optional ?threshold=critical query parameter...', 'Add integration tests for the new endpoint covering: successful aggregation with correct counts, 404 for missing SBOM, deduplication of advisories, cache header verification, and threshold filtering'. Each change names specific structs, methods, endpoints, tables, and test scenarios.

assertion_results.evidence: All 6 tasks have a '## Repository' section containing exactly one repository name 'trustify-backend' — no task lists multiple repositories. Verified in task-1 line 3-4, task-2 line 3-4, task-3 line 3-4, task-4 line 3-4, task-5 line 3-4, task-6 line 3-4.

assertion_results.text: After each task is created, a description digest comment is posted with a format-tagged SHA-256 hash — exactly 64 lowercase hex characters prefixed by 'sha256-md:' or 'sha256-adf:', not a placeholder, abbreviated value, or example string. Marker format: '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:<64-char-hex>' (or sha256-adf). The digest is computed by re-fetching the description from the API and running scripts/sha256-digest.py
assertion_results.evidence: Searched all output files for 'sha256', 'digest', and 'Description digest' — no matches found in any of the 7 output files (impact-map.md, task-1 through task-6). No description digest comments are present in the outputs directory.

assertion_results.text: Convention-aware enrichment validates file-type applicability per shared/convention-applicability-rules.md before including a convention — inapplicable conventions are excluded entirely (not listed with 'Not applicable' annotations), and applicable ones include a rationale in the prescribed format ('Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>'), not free-form prose
assertion_results.evidence: Searched all 6 task files for 'CONVENTIONS', 'Applies:', and convention-related content. No task contains any reference to CONVENTIONS.md sections or convention applicability rationales in the prescribed format ('Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>'). The only convention-related references are to docs/constraints.md sections (e.g., 'Per docs/constraints.md Section 5 (Code Change Rules)' and 'Per docs/constraints.md Section 2 (Commit Rules)'), which are not the same as CONVENTIONS.md convention enrichment. No convention-aware enrichment with applicability validation was performed.
